DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DANIEL FEDER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - The court overrules the demurrer to the 6th cause of action for violation of Labor 203 [waiting time penalties]. Paragraphs. 28 and 29 of the complaint sufficiently allege that the condition precedent (settlement of class action and payment to defendant) occurred and plaintiff is entitled to payment. Discovery will reveal the precise dates that payment became due. The court sustains the demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation cause of action without leave to amend. The misrepresentation that defendant would pay plaintiff a certain sum relates to a future event and is not actionable. "To be actionable, a negligent misrepresentation must ordinarily be as to past or existing material facts. '[P]redictions as to future events, or statements as to future action by some third party, are deemed opinions, and not actionable fraud.'" Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 158. The court overrules the demurrer to the UCL cause of action under Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 163, 173. That case clearly holds that a party can recover unlawfully withheld wages as restitution under the UCL. The court overrules the demurrer to the promissory estoppel cause of action because defendant's moving papers address a different claim. The court overrules the demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action. Plaintiff is seeking prospective relief because some of the class actions that plaintiff worked on are still settling. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DANIEL FEDER'S MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IN THE COMPLAINT is denied. The conversion cause of action (a tort) supports the claim for punitive damages. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ehg
